summary: Implemented a vendored ylong_runtime readiness scheduling candidate that avoids waking another worker for same-runtime local FIFO wake_by_ref enqueue when the local queue is not full. The candidate is behavior-preserving for HTTP/TLS/proxy semantics and is S9C evidence only; S9D canonical SOTA matrix remains pending.

candidate.name: same-runtime local FIFO enqueue wake suppression
causal_basis: S9B found that ylong_base/tokio_base have the same HTTP response poll count and the same nested TLS pending shape.
S9B found ylong-specific worker migration and strong response-wait correlation with park and steal/search activity.

Source review showed TaskHandle::wake_by_ref schedules with lifo=false, and MultiThreadScheduler::enqueue_under_ctx previously returned true after ordinary same-runtime local FIFO enqueue, causing schedule() to call wake_up_rand_one(false).
candidate.behavior_change: For same-runtime worker context and lifo=false, enqueue_under_ctx now returns false after local queue enqueue unless the local queue was already full and may overflow to the global queue.
preserved_behaviors: Foreign-runtime context still enqueues to the global queue and returns true.
No-worker context still enqueues to the global queue and returns true.
LIFO scheduling behavior is kept unchanged, including the existing return true path when replacing an occupied lifo slot.
A full local queue conservatively returns true so global overflow can still wake another worker.
HTTP, TLS, proxy CONNECT formatting, parsing, mTLS, and origin/proxy TLS configuration code are not changed.

workspace_patch.dependency_tree_result: ylong_http and ylong_http_client both resolve to the same vendored ylong_runtime path; no duplicate runtime crate was observed.

runtime_files.change: Added a unit test and changed same-runtime FIFO local enqueue wake hint from unconditional true to true only when local queue remaining capacity is zero.
Applied the Rust 1.96 dangerous_implicit_autorefs compatibility fix needed to compile the vendored runtime; this makes implicit raw-pointer autorefs explicit and does not alter scheduling behavior.

dependency_tree.command: source ~/.cargo/env && cargo tree -p ylong_http_client --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0 --offline | rg -n "ylong_runtime|ylong_io|ylong_runtime_macros|https://gitee"

commands.name: CONNECT parser and 2xx tunnel response semantics
commands.command: source ~/.cargo/env && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_s9c cargo test -p ylong_http_client --lib --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0 ut_tunnel_request_and_response --offline -- --nocapture

commands.name: async HTTPS proxy TLS and mTLS E2E
commands.command: source ~/.cargo/env && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_s9c cargo test -p ylong_http_client --test sdv_async_https_proxy_tls --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,tokio_base,c_openssl_3_0 -- --nocapture --test-threads=1

non_gating_observations.name: http3-over-proxy reject unit
non_gating_observations.command: source ~/.cargo/env && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_s9c_h3 RUSTFLAGS='-C link-arg=/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libssl.so -C link-arg=/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libcrypto.so' cargo test -p ylong_http_client --lib --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,http3,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0 ut_http3_over_proxy_is_rejected -- --nocapture
non_gating_observations.result: failed before reaching S9C runtime behavior
non_gating_observations.failure: Existing test mock TestStream in ylong_http_client/src/async_impl/pool.rs does not implement ConnInfo::quic_conn under the http3 test feature set.
non_gating_observations.gate_effect: Not used as S9C pass evidence; S9C does not modify http3 proxy rejection code.

smoke_benchmark.purpose: S9C real-path smoke only; not the S9D canonical SOTA matrix.
smoke_benchmark.command: python docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --requests 32 --repeats 1 --warmup 8 --concurrency 8 --baseline libcurl --ylong-client async --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --scenario https-over-https-proxy --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_s9c_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/ylong-runtime-readiness-candidate-smoke --figure-dir docs/figures/ylong-runtime-readiness-candidate-smoke

s9c_verdict.reason: The candidate is implemented and smoke-verified, but the approved canonical matrix and final counterexample gate have not run.
